title: pulmonaria azurea subsp. vulgaris
intro: Pulmonaria azurea subsp. vulgaris, also known as Lungwort, is a perennial plant belonging to the borage family, native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and expectorant effects, making it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant is rich in saponins, flavonoids, and iridoids, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory function. Pulmonaria azurea subsp. vulgaris can be prepared into various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
